task_completion.description: The original image is opened in GIMP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP interface is visible with an image loaded in the main canvas.

task_completion.description: The Pixelize filter dialog is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box titled 'Pixelize' is visible over the GIMP interface (typically accessed via Filters -> Blur -> Pixelize).

task_completion.description: The Pixelize filter settings are configured to 10x10 blocks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Pixelize dialog, the 'Block width' and 'Block height' values are set to 10.

task_completion.description: The mosaic effect is applied to the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image in the GIMP canvas appears pixelated, consisting of uniform square color blocks.

task_completion.description: The modified image is exported as 'pixelated_image.png'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Export Image' dialog is visible with the filename 'pixelated_image.png', or an icon for 'pixelated_image.png' is visible on the Desktop.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP GUI to apply the filter and export the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GIMP menus and dialogs, rather than terminal windows running ImageMagick or Python scripts.

integrity.description: The mosaic effect is applied to the original image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The pixelated image retains the overall composition and colors of the original image, rather than being a completely different image or a blank canvas.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires applying the Pixelize filter in GIMP (Filters -> Blur -> Pixelize) with a block size of 10x10 pixels. The final image must be exported as 'pixelated_image.png' to the Desktop (/home/ga/Desktop/). The verifier checks for a mosaic effect with uniform color blocks (variance reduction and block uniformity).
